title: iberis carnosa subsp. rhomarensis
intro: Iberis carnosa subsp. rhomarensis, also known as rosemary rock candy, is a subspecies of the Iberis carnosa plant, belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities. The plant is used to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of Iberis carnosa subsp. rhomarensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections.
